title: Transient Artifact
description: Base schema for Archeia transient artifacts — tasks, drafts, plans, and running experiments that flow through lifecycle states and are pruned after a bounded retention window.

id.description: Stable identifier for this artifact across its lifecycle.

author.description: Who (human or agent) created the artifact.

status.description: Distribution-defined status value. Every status value must map to exactly one temporal category (future, present, past) via the distribution's lifecycles.yaml or equivalent.

started.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the artifact advanced from a future-mapped status to a present-mapped status.

terminal_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the artifact reached a past-mapped status. The field name in the actual artifact is distribution-defined (e.g., 'completed', 'cancelled_at', 'concluded', 'discarded_at') but the semantic role is this: it starts the retention clock.
